agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-bound SLA, premium pricing, and refund policy for missed windows. Spans multiple bounded contexts: order placement, fulfillment/dispatch, driver management, billing/refunds, and customer tracking.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — cuts across Order, Fulfillment, Driver, Billing, and Tracking contexts. This analysis treats Express Delivery as a subdomain within a broader logistics platform.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option with a target completion time of 2 hours from order placement, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"A fixed surcharge of $12.99 charged for selecting the Express Delivery option."},{"term":"Delivery Window","definition":"The 2-hour period starting from order placement within which the express delivery should be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to physical pickup of the package by a driver."},{"term":"Express-Eligible Driver","definition":"A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is qualified to fulfill express deliveries."},{"term":"Service Radius","definition":"The 10km maximum distance between customer delivery address and the nearest warehouse, determining express eligibility."},{"term":"Missed Window","definition":"When an express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering refund evaluation."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"A discretionary (non-contractual) refund of the express fee issued when a delivery window is missed, subject to exclusion conditions."},{"term":"Driver Cancellation","definition":"When an assigned express driver abandons or cancels a delivery mid-fulfillment, triggering replacement assignment."},{"term":"Replacement Window","definition":"The 10-minute period after a driver cancellation within which a new express-eligible driver must be assigned."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery charge that is never refunded under the express refund policy."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — fulfillment within 2 hours is impossible beyond service radius. Violating this corrupts the eligibility model.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery address >10km from all warehouses, the system must not allow express delivery selection."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"The express fee is $12.99.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy that can change with a pricing update.","testable_assertion":"When a customer selects express delivery, the express fee line item equals $12.99."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An express order must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Operations states the 2-hour window cannot be met if pickup exceeds 15 minutes — this is a physical/operational constraint that protects the SLA.","testable_assertion":"If no driver has picked up the order within 15 minutes of placement, the system flags the order as pickup-SLA-breached."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Driver quality directly affects delivery reliability. Assigning unqualified drivers corrupts fulfillment integrity.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver with a rating below 4.0, the system must not assign them to an express order."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When the delivery window is missed, the express fee is refunded but the base delivery fee is not.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund scope is a business policy decision, changeable by finance/leadership.","testable_assertion":"Given a missed delivery window with no exclusions, the refund amount equals the express fee only, not the base delivery fee."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Refunds are not issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund exclusion is a business policy.","testable_assertion":"Given a missed delivery window where the address was flagged as customer-error, no refund is issued."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds for missed windows are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Processing timeline is an operational SLA, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an approved refund, the refund transaction is initiated within 3 business days of approval."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement express-eligible driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Replacement window is an operational target. The 10-minute threshold could be adjusted.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver cancellation on an express order, a replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes or the order is escalated."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"If no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes of a driver cancellation, the express fee is automatically refunded.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Auto-refund trigger is a customer success policy, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a driver cancellation where 10 minutes elapse with no replacement, the system automatically initiates an express fee refund."},{"id":"BR-10","rule":"Express customers can track their assigned driver in real time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an in-progress express delivery, the customer can view the driver's current location updating in near real-time."},{"id":"BR-11","rule":"The express delivery time commitment is a best-effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Legal constraint — representing this as a guarantee exposes the company to liability. All customer-facing language must reflect this.","testable_assertion":"No customer-facing text, contract, or communication uses the word 'guarantee' in relation to the 2-hour delivery window."}],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer is exactly 10km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — need to clarify whether 10km is inclusive or exclusive boundary. Recommend inclusive (<=10km)."},{"scenario":"Pickup SLA breached but delivery still completes within 2 hours","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — does pickup SLA breach trigger any action (refund, escalation) independently, or only if the delivery window is also missed?"},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at minute 110 of a 120-minute window","expected_behavior":"Replacement window of 10 minutes extends beyond the delivery window. Is the refund triggered by missed window or by failed replacement? Both rules may fire simultaneously."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address after order placement to a location >10km","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — does the order lose express status? Is the fee refunded? What if the new address causes a missed window?"},{"scenario":"Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars while on an active express delivery","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — is the driver removed mid-delivery or allowed to complete the current order?"},{"scenario":"No express-eligible drivers available at time of order placement","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — should express option be hidden, or shown with a warning? Should the order queue and wait?"},{"scenario":"Wrong address determination is disputed by customer","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — who adjudicates? What evidence is required? Is the refund held during dispute?"},{"scenario":"Multiple driver cancellations on the same order","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — does each cancellation restart the 10-minute replacement clock? Is there a maximum retry count before automatic cancellation and full refund?"},{"scenario":"Warehouse goes offline or runs out of stock after express order placed","expected_behavior":"UNDEFINED — this is a fulfillment failure, not a driver failure. Refund policy for operational failures not addressed."},{"scenario":"3 business days for refund processing vs automatic refund on replacement failure","expected_behavior":"Potential tension — auto-refund implies immediate processing, but Finance says 3 business days. Need to clarify if auto-refund bypasses the 3-day processing window."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Express fee at $12.99 creates a new revenue stream. Refund exposure is bounded to the express fee only (base fee protected). However, auto-refund triggers (missed window, replacement failure) could erode margins if operational SLAs are frequently missed. Need data on expected miss rate to model refund liability.","operational":"15-minute pickup SLA requires real-time dispatch optimization and sufficient express-eligible driver pool. Driver rating threshold of 4.0 reduces the eligible pool. Replacement within 10 minutes demands standby capacity or dynamic reallocation. Real-time tracking requires GPS infrastructure investment.","user":"Premium experience expectation — customers paying $12.99 expect reliability. The tension between marketing it prominently and Legal's best-effort stance creates a trust risk. Real-time tracking adds transparency but also exposes delays visibly.","risk":"HIGH — Legal contradiction on guarantee language is the top risk. If customer-facing copy says 'guaranteed' while Legal says 'best effort,' the company faces regulatory and litigation exposure. Secondary risk: if the express-eligible driver pool is too small, chronic SLA misses will make refunds a cost center rather than edge case.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"New premium option with faster delivery. Risk of frustration if 'guarantee' framing sets expectations that best-effort SLA cannot meet."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"New tier of assignments with rating gate. Drivers below 4.0 lose access to express orders (and associated incentives, if any). Drivers face pressure from 15-minute pickup SLA."},{"group":"Operations/Dispatch","impact":"Significant complexity increase — must support real-time SLA monitoring, 15-minute pickup enforcement, 10-minute replacement assignment, and express-eligible driver pool management."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"New revenue line, new refund liability. Must build refund processing pipeline with exclusion logic and 3-business-day SLA tracking."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Must review ALL customer-facing language to ensure no contractual guarantee is implied. Refund framed as goodwill, not obligation."},{"group":"Customer Success","impact":"Will handle disputes on wrong-address refund exclusions and missed-window complaints. Need clear escalation paths and adjudication criteria."},{"group":"Engineering","impact":"Must build: eligibility check (distance calculation), driver rating gate, real-time tracking, pickup SLA monitoring, replacement assignment engine, auto-refund trigger, refund exclusion logic."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct contradiction between PM ('guaranteed') and Legal ('best effort / not a guarantee') that fundamentally affects the product's identity, customer-facing language, refund obligations, and acceptance criteria. Until this is resolved, acceptance criteria cannot be reliably defined. Additionally, multiple edge cases have no defined behavior, and the interaction between auto-refund triggers and the 3-business-day processing window is unclear.","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99)","10km service radius eligibility","Driver rating threshold (4.0 stars)","Base delivery fee is never refunded","Wrong address excludes refund eligibility"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort — contradicted by PM vs Legal","Auto-refund timing vs 3-business-day processing window","What happens when pickup SLA is breached but delivery window is not","Replacement failure escalation path beyond the first cancellation","Address dispute adjudication process"],"assumptions":["The 10km radius is calculated from the nearest warehouse, not a specific warehouse","Driver rating is a cumulative platform rating, not an express-specific rating","'Order placement' means the moment payment is confirmed, not cart creation","'Real time' tracking means sub-30-second location updates","The 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"PM states the 2-hour delivery is 'guaranteed' and the refund is automatic on miss. Legal explicitly states this is a 'best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee' and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These are irreconcilable — the product cannot be both guaranteed and best-effort. This affects all customer-facing language, refund policy framing, and potentially whether refunds are automatic or discretionary.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Legal","Product Management","Marketing","Customer Success"],"options":["A: Adopt Legal's position — frame as best-effort with discretionary goodwill refund. Remove 'guarantee' from all materials.","B: Adopt PM's position — treat as a guarantee with automatic refund. Legal must accept the liability exposure.","C: Hybrid — internal SLA is a guarantee (auto-refund on miss), but customer-facing language uses 'commitment' or 'target' instead of 'guarantee' to limit legal exposure."],"recommendation":"Option C — operational behavior matches PM's intent (auto-refund on miss) while customer-facing language satisfies Legal. However, Legal must explicitly approve the specific language used."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Customer Success says refund is 'automatic' on replacement failure. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' It is unclear whether auto-refund means instant credit or simply auto-approved but processed within 3 days.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance","Customer Success","Engineering"],"options":["A: Auto-refund means instant credit to customer payment method.","B: Auto-refund means auto-approved (no manual review) but processed within standard 3-business-day window."],"recommendation":"Option B — auto-approval removes friction while preserving Finance's processing timeline. But Customer Success should confirm this meets their intent."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No stakeholder addressed what happens when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached. Is the order cancelled? Downgraded to standard? Does the customer get notified? Is a refund triggered immediately or only if the 2-hour window is also missed?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Operations","Engineering"],"options":["A: Pickup SLA breach auto-cancels express, downgrades to standard, refunds express fee.","B: Pickup SLA breach triggers escalation but order remains express — refund only if 2-hour window ultimately missed.","C: Pickup SLA breach notifies customer and offers choice: wait or downgrade."],"recommendation":"Option A — if Operations says 2 hours cannot be met without 15-minute pickup, then breaching pickup SLA should proactively refund rather than waiting to fail the delivery window."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No information on driver incentives or compensation for express deliveries. If drivers are held to higher standards (4.0+ rating, 15-minute pickup), there should be a corresponding incentive structure, otherwise driver adoption will be low.","affected_stakeholders":["Drivers","Operations","Finance"],"options":["Define express delivery premium for drivers","Rely on existing compensation model"],"recommendation":"Define an express driver premium — without it, the eligible driver pool may be insufficient to meet demand, causing chronic SLA misses and refund liability."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No definition of how 'wrong address' is determined or disputed. This is the sole refund exclusion and will be a major source of customer complaints if not clearly defined.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Customer Success","Legal"],"options":["A: Wrong address = address does not exist or is unreachable (objective criteria).","B: Wrong address = any address discrepancy including typos, apartment numbers, etc.","C: Driver marks address as wrong, customer can dispute within N days."],"recommendation":"Need explicit criteria. Recommend objective definition (Option A) with a dispute mechanism (Option C) to avoid arbitrary refund denials."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot define acceptance criteria while 'guarantee vs best-effort' contradiction is unresolved.","business_rule_broken":"BR-11 (Legal: best effort) directly contradicts PM framing and Customer Success auto-refund expectations. The entire refund policy hinges on whether this is a guarantee or not."}]}
